commit_scope: Answer 'how do users become git members automatically?' in the body's frame: they don't, and they don't need to. Ships membership-as-recognition.fk — the arrival gate that routes a stranger to fork-pr-or-offer (no write grant), attributes their trace to a held-open placeholder (Contributor.claimed=false), and keeps two hard invariants: never auto-grant repo write, never auto-bind a name to a contributor cell. Proven four-way at 511. Plus the teaching and the AGENTS.md wiring so the agent walks the arc.
evidence_refs: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/membership-as-recognition.fk form-stdlib/tests/membership-as-recognition-band.fk -> '✓ ... → 511' and 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)' and '1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree on every sample.'
Manifest row added: 'membership-as-recognition fks 511' beside its kin freq-aligned-share.

The band pins the gate: a stranger (no write, unbound, returned a trace, consents) -> path fork-pr-or-offer, state placeholder, auto-write 0, auto-bind 0, participates-no-grant 1 (the whole point); a member (bound + consent) -> claimed; a reader -> read-only/none; a writer -> direct, yet auto-write is STILL 0 (the gate granted nothing). Verdict 511.

Grounding (Form-first, body read): the safe shape already exists — api/app/models/contributor.py Contributor.claimed (false on held-open placeholder) + canonical_url; api/app/routers/contributions.py record_open_contribution; contributor_recognition / contributor_identity routers; public-offer-lane.form (a stranger offers, grounding is recognition not arrival). This gate names + proves the arc over them; no parallel machinery.

Edges in the same commit: docs/coherence-substrate/INDEX.md row beside public-offer-lane; AGENTS.md bullet so the agent walks the arc (fork+PR on the user's identity, record the trace, attribute to a placeholder, invite the claim, never bind for them).
Primitive discipline held: only core.fk primitives (nth, eq, and binary-nested, if, str_eq, add); no sub/mul/div, no 3-arg and.

Why this is the build, not the literal ask: auto-granting git write to strangers is an unsafe outward effect on shared state; auto-binding a name is the exact Robin/Aly failure. The gate delivers full participation + attribution while refusing both — more sovereign than 'automatic membership'.

fourth_arm_outputs: membership-as-recognition: verdict 511, fourth arm 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree

ci_validation.notes: CI re-runs validate.sh across the suite for the new recipe + band + manifest row.

deploy_validation.notes: A Form stdlib recipe + proof band + a teaching + agent guidance; no public HTTP endpoint or service change. The gate reaches agents through the repo. Wiring the gate into a live contribution route is a named next step, not this change.

phase_gate.reason: Local four-way validation passed (verdict 511, 0 divergent); CI and merge remain pending. No deploy applies to a Form stdlib recipe.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The contribution arc for a stranger is now named and proven: fork-pr-or-offer (no write grant) -> attributed to a held-open placeholder (claimed:false) -> claimed by the person when they choose. The gate refuses auto-write-grant and auto-identity-bind whatever the inputs. AGENTS.md tells the agent to walk it.
public_endpoints: none changed; the gate is a Form stdlib body proven by validate.sh; the existing contribution/recognition doors are its carriers
test_flows: Four-kernel proof: the band runs on Go, Rust, TypeScript, and fkwu to the identical verdict 511 with 0 divergent.
Invariant proof: even a write-holding arrival yields auto-write 0 (the gate grants nothing); an unbound trace yields a placeholder, never a bound name.
